title: Anti-spam PoW enrollment gate
description:
## Why

Currently anyone can authenticate and broadcast for free (or at minimal Zcash cost when REQUIRE_SUBSCRIPTION=false). A subscribed attacker can automate thousands of accounts. PoW raises per-account compute cost without requiring any PII.

## What

Add a double-SHA256 Hashcash-style PoW challenge to the authentication flow:

- Hash input: `ver || op_type || ts_floor || diff || hex(ed25519_pub) || server_salt || nonce_be64`
- Token: binary struct (ver u8, op_type u8, ts_floor u32, diff u8, nonce u64, h16 [u8;16]) = 31 bytes, base64url-encoded
- Default difficulty: 20-bit leading zeros; stored in AppState, adjustable at runtime without restart
- Staleness window: ±600s from ts_floor * 60 (prevents pre-mined tokens)
- Replay prevention: store h16 in an in-memory set (BTreeSet or DashMap) keyed by h16, TTL = 600s
- Client mines in a background thread (rayon or std::thread) before sending Authenticate

## Acceptance Criteria

- Relay issues a PoW challenge before accepting Authenticate
- Tokens with wrong difficulty, stale timestamp, or replayed h16 are rejected with AuthFailed
- Difficulty is configurable via POW_DIFFICULTY env var without restart
- nie-cli mines the PoW automatically before chatting (user sees no extra step)
- Unit test: valid token accepted; wrong difficulty, stale, and replayed tokens all rejected
- DESIGN.md §PoW invariants document the token format as a stable API
close_reason: PoW enrollment gate complete: pow.rs module, ws.rs verification, transport.rs mining, E2E tests, DESIGN.md
